Python library for Synthetic Data Generation

This is part of the public API.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.utilblocks.FlattenColumnsBlock
documentation:
Melt/transform a data from a wide format to a long format see pandas.melt for a description

Args:
        var_cols (list): Column(s) to unpivot. All other columns are set to use as identifier variables.
        value_name (str): Name to use for the ‘value’ column, can’t be an existing column label.
        var_name (str):  Name to use for the ‘variable’ column. If None it uses frame.columns.name or ‘variable’.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utilblocks.DuplicateColumnsBlock.__init__
documentation:
Create duplicate of columns specified in column map.

Args:
    columns_map (dict): mapping of existing column to new column names

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utilblocks.RenameColumnsBlock.__init__
documentation:
Rename dataset columns.

Args:
    columns_map (dict): mapping of existing column to new column names

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.utilblocks.SetToMajorityValueBlock
documentation:
Set the value of the specified column to the most common value (the mode)

Args:
    col_name (str): the column to find the "mode" of and then set universally

modules.name: instructlab.sdg.generate_data
documentation: SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
Standard

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.generate_data._convert_to_messages
documentation:
Convert a sample dictionary to contain 'messages' and 'metadata' columns required for training.

Note that this is for the legacy messages format, used before data
mixing was introduced. Once we can drop the older `messages_*.jsonl`
output files, this can go away.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.generate_data._gen_train_data
documentation:
Generate training data in the legacy system/user/assistant format
used in train_*.jsonl as well as the legacy messages format used
in messages_*.jsonl files.

This can be dropped once we no longer need those formats and are fully
using the new data mixing messages format.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.generate_data._gen_test_data
documentation:
Generate test data in the format needed by the legacy Linux training
in instructlab/instructlab.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.generate_data.generate_data
documentation:
Generate data for training and testing a model.

This currently serves as the primary interface from the `ilab` CLI to the `sdg` library.
It is somewhat a transitionary measure, as this function existed back when all of the
functionality was embedded in the CLI. At some stage, we expect to evolve the CLI to
use the SDG library constructs directly, and this function will likely be removed.

Args:
    pipeline: This argument may be either an alias defined in a user or site "data directory"
              or an alias defined by the sdg library ("simple", "full")(if the data directory has no matches),
              or an absolute path to a directory containing the pipeline YAML files.
              We expect three files to be present in this directory: "knowledge.yaml",
                "freeform_skills.yaml", and "grounded_skills.yaml".
This is part of the public API, and used by instructlab.
TODO - parameter removal needs to be done in sync with a CLI change.
to be removed: logger, prompt_file_path, rouge_threshold, tls_*

modules.name: instructlab.sdg.block
documentation: SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
Standard

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.block.Block

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.block.Block._load_config
documentation:
Load the configuration file for this block.

If the supplied configuration file is a relative path, it is assumed
to be part of this Python package.

```
param config_path

The path to the configuration file.

return

The loaded configuration.
```
This is part of the public API.

modules.name: instructlab.sdg.checkpointing
documentation: SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
Standard

modules.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing
documentation: SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
Standard

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._adjust_train_sample_size
documentation:
Return a dataset with num_samples random samples selected from the
original dataset.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._sample_ds
documentation:
Select sampling_size number/ratio of samples from a dataset, ensuring
the returned dataset has only ALLOWED_COLS columns in it with any
additional columns moved to the metadata section.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._add_system_message
documentation: Ensure every sample has a system message with the correct system prompt

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._get_question_hack
documentation: This is a hack because the simple workflow returns a q/a pair as a single output.
We could possibly try to ask for them separately, but it would cost twice the inference
API calls. All of this is because the smallest models we use on small environments
for testing and demos weren't good enough to follow the strict formatting instructions used
in the full pipeline.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._get_response_hack
documentation: This is also a hack. See the comment above _get_question.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._generate_knowledge_qa_dataset
documentation:
Generate question and answer pairs from the newly generated dataset
for each taxonomy leaf node. Each row of the generated dataset gets
converted to have messages, metadata, and id columns.

If `keep_context_separate` is True, then a context column is also added.
If `keep_context_separate` is False, the context colum is omitted and
the context is instead added directly to the user message content.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._add_extra_contexts_to_samples
documentation:
Add additional context to each sample in a knowledge_qa_dataset by
selecting the context from random other samples and adding that
combined with this sample's original context all into the user content
section of the sample's messages.

This expects to be called with a dataset that has a `context` column,
such as the output from _generate_knowledge_qa_dataset with the param
`keep_context_separate` equal to True. When this finishes, the `context`
column is removed from the dataset and all context moved to the user
messages.

This is inspired by the concepts of Retrieval Augmented FineTuning (RAFT)
from https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.10131

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._conv_pretrain
documentation:
Convert a messages dataset that contains only user/assistant entries per
message (and in that order) to a pretraining message used downstream by
the training pipeline. `_generate_knowledge_qa_dataset` creates the type
of dataset expected here.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._create_phase10_ds
documentation:
Create a dataset for Phase 1.0 of downstream training.

This dataset is in our messages format, with each sample having
additional context mixed in from other samples to improve the
training outcomes.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._create_phase07_ds
documentation:
Create a dataset for Phase 0.7 of downstream training.

Phase 0.7 is a pretraining phase, and this dataset contains messages
with a special `pretraining` role used by downstream training before
running the full training with the Phase 1.0 dataset.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._total_length_of_datasets
documentation:
Iterate through the datasets and return the total number of samples
per the the sampling ratio.

Args:
    datasets (List[DatasetListing]): List containing `DatasetListing` entries.

Returns:
    int: Combined length of all datasets in the given list.

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing._convert_to_leaf_node_messages
documentation:
Convert a sample dictionary to contain a 'messages' column required
for training.

Note that this is for the new messages format, introduced with data
mixing.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.DatasetListing
documentation:
TypedDict class that represents the dataset listings passed around in the
`.datasets` key of each recipe.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.Recipe

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.Recipe._load_ds
documentation:
Load a dataset from the given location. If a jsonl file is
given, we load the dataset from disk. Otherwise, we load the
path given from HuggingFace. Relative paths are resolved
respective to the directory the recipe yaml itself resides in.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.Recipe._load_and_sample_datasets
documentation:
Load and sample all the datasets in this recipe, taking
into account the desired sampling size from each individual
dataset to control the overall mix of samples in the final
dataset.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.Recipe._create_mixed_dataset
documentation:
Create the final mixed dataset by loading, sampling, and
concatenating all datasets in this recipe

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.Recipe.add_dataset
documentation:
Add a dataset to this recipe.

Args:
    path: The file path to the dataset's samples, as jsonl
    sampling_size: An int or float that specifies the number of
                   samples (if int) or the ratio of samples (if
                   float) to include in the mixed dataset. A value
                   of 1.0 means include all samples, 0.5 means half
                   of the samples, and so on.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.Recipe.save_mixed_dataset
documentation:
Create the mixed dataset and write it to the specified output path
as a jsonl file.

A Recipe describes how datasets were mixed, including the path and
sampling size used for each included dataset as well as the system
prompt used when generating the data in those datasets.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.DataMixer._load_default_recipe
documentation:
Load a default system recipe from e.g. /usr/share/instructlab/sdg/default_data_recipes
if it exists, otherwise return an empty recipe.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.DataMixer._gen_leaf_node_data
documentation:
Write the data generated from each taxonomy leaf node to a file.
Later on, after all data is generated, the data mixing will read data
from these files to generate the overall mixed dataset.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.datamixing.DataMixer._gen_mixed_data
documentation:
Mix the generated leaf node data into a single dataset and write it to
disk. The heavy lifting is delegated to the Recipe class.

modules.name: instructlab.sdg.pipeline
documentation: SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
Standard

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.pipeline.EmptyDatasetError
documentation: This is part of the public API.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.pipeline.PipelineContext

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.pipeline.PipelineContext.batching_enabled
documentation:
Batching is enabled IFF the batch size is specified and the number of
workers is not set explicitly to 1

A PipelineContext holds the common attributes needed between blocks in a
pipeline

client: The OpenAI client handle.
model_id: The ID of the teacher model to be used for client calls.
model_family: The family identifier for the model being updated.
num_instructions_to_generate: The total number of instructions the user
    wants to generate during this run.
batch_size: The size of the dataset batches for parallel generation. Set to
    0 to disable batching.
batch_num_workers: The number of worker threads/processes to maintain in the
    central executor pool.
dataset_num_procs: The number of processes to use when performing parallel
   map operations on individual datasets.
max_num_tokens: the maximum number of tokens to generate per sample.
This is part of the public API.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.pipeline.PipelineBlockError
documentation:
A PipelineBlockError occurs when a block generates an exception during
generation. It contains information about which block failed and why.
This is part of the public API.

Filters out badly generated data, adds dataset type column

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.llmblock.LLMBlock._format_prompt
documentation: There are three cases to handle for self.model_prompt
1. None - no model_prompt specified, look one up based on model family
2. Non-empty string - the pipeline has specified a custom model prompt
3. Empty string - the pipeline has specified that no model prompt is needed

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.llmblock.LLMBlock.generate
documentation:
Generate the output from the block. This method should first validate the input data,
then generate the output, and finally parse the generated output before returning it.

```
return

The parsed output after generation.
```

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.llmblock.LLMBlock._validate
documentation:
Validate the input data for this block. This method should be implemented by subclasses
to define how the block validates its input data.

```
return

True if the input data is valid, False otherwise.
```
This is part of the public API.
pylint: disable=dangerous-default-value

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.taxonomy._get_documents
documentation:
Retrieve the content of files (Markdown and PDF) from a Git repository.

Args:
    source (dict): Source info containing repository URL, commit hash, and list of file patterns.
    skip_checkout (bool, optional): If True, skips checking out the specific commit. Defaults to False.
    document_output_dir (Path, optional): Directory to clone the repository into. Defaults to current directory.

Returns:
    Tuple[List[str], List[Path]]:
        - List of document contents (Markdown as text and PDFs as extracted text).
        - List of corresponding file paths.

Raises:
    SystemExit: If no valid documents are found.
    OSError, GitCommandError, FileNotFoundError: For errors during Git operations or file access.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.filterblock.FilterByValueBlockError
documentation: An exception raised by the FilterByValue block.
This is part of the public API.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.filterblock.FilterByValueBlock

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.filterblock.FilterByValueBlock.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new instance of the FilterByValueBlock class.

Parameters:
- ctx (PipelineContext): A PipelineContext object containing runtime parameters.
- pipe (Pipeline): The Pipeline containing this block in its chain.
- block_name (str): An identifier for this block.
- filter_column (str): The name of the column in the dataset to apply the filter on.
- filter_value (any or list of any): The value(s) to filter by.
- operation (string): The name of a function provided by the "operator"
  Python package that takes two arguments (column value and filter value)
  and returns a boolean indicating whether the row should be included in
  the filtered dataset.
- convert_dtype (string, optional): the name of a Python type to convert
  the column values to. Supported values are "int", "float", and "bool".
  Defaults to None.
- default_value (string, optional): a default value that should be used
  if convert_dtype fails. Defaults to 0 for int/float, and False for bool.

Returns:
None

For supported values of `operation`, see the "operator" package
documentation: https://docs.python.org/3/library/operator.html

Only a subset of the "operator" package is relevant. It has to
follow the semantics of taking two parameters and returning a boolean.
Some operations that work include:
- eq: equal to
- ne: not equal to
- gt: greater than
- ge: greater than or equal to
- lt: less than
- le: less than or equal to
- contains: filter_column contains filter_value (only for string columns)

Note that the semantics of all operations are:
  - filter_column operation filter_value

Example: FilterByValueBlock(ctx, "filter_by_age", "age", 30, "eq", "int")
    - This block will filter the dataset to only include rows where the
      "age" column is equal to 30.

The `contains` operator is only supported for string columns. This is
useful if you want to ensure that a string column contains a specific
substring.

Example: FilterByValueBlock(ctx, "filter_by_name", "full_name", "John", "contains")
    - This block will filter the dataset to only include rows where the
      "full_name" column contains the substring "John".

`filter_value` does not have to be a single value. It can also be a list of values.
In that case, the operation will be applied to each value in the list. The result is
considered True if the operation is True for any of the values in the list.

Example: FilterByValueBlock(ctx, "filter_by_age", "age", [30, 35], "eq", "int", "0")
    - This block will filter the dataset to only include rows where the
      "age" column is equal to 30 or 35. Non-integer values will be treated like zero.

Example: FilterByValueBlock(ctx, "filter_by_city", "city", ["boston", "charleston", "dublin", "new york"], "eq")
    - This block will filter the dataset to only include rows where the
      "city" column is equal to "boston", "charleston", "dublin", or "new york".

Example: FilterByValueBlock(ctx, "filter_by_name", "full_name", ["John", "Jane"], "contains")
    - This block will filter the dataset to only include rows where the
      "full_name" column contains the substring "John" or "Jane".
This is part of the public API.

modules.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers
documentation: Standard

functions.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.chunk_markdowns
documentation:
Iterates over the documents and splits them into chunks based on the word count provided by the user.
Args:
    documents (list): List of documents retrieved from git (can also consist of a single document).
    server_ctx_size (int): Context window size of server.
    chunk_word_count (int): Maximum number of words to chunk a document.
Returns:
     List[str]: List of chunked documents.

classes.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.DocumentChunker

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.DocumentChunker.__new__
documentation:
Insantiate the appropriate chunker for the provided document

Args:
    leaf_node: a leaf node dict containing "documents",
        "filepaths", and "taxonomy_path" keys
    output_dir (Path): directory where artifacts should be stored
    server_ctx_size (int): Context window size of server
    chunk_word_count (int): Maximum number of words to chunk a document
    tokenizer_model_name (Optional[str]): name of huggingface model to get
        tokenizer from
Returns:
    TextSplitChunker | ContextAwareChunker: Object of the appropriate
        chunker class for the provided filetype

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.DocumentChunker._split_docs_by_filetype
documentation:
Separate documents into lists based on their filetype.

Currently, only Markdown and PDF are supported.
Args:
    documents (List[str]): A list of the document contents as strings
    filepaths (List[Path]): Corresponding document filepaths
Returns:
    DefaultDict: Dictionary with either ".md" or ".pdf" as a key.
        Markdown items contain document contents, PDF items contain
        paths to documents.

A factory chunker class that instantiates the applicable chunker

Currently, only Markdown and PDF are supported. For Markdown, returns
TextSplitChunker, and for PDF, returns ContextAwareChunker

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.TextSplitChunker.chunk_documents
documentation:
Naively chunk markdown documents based on the word count provided by the user.
Returns:
    List[str]: List of chunked documents.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.chunk_documents
documentation:
Semantically chunk PDF documents.

Returns:
    List: a list of chunks from the documents

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker._path_validator
documentation:
Validate the path and return a Path object.
Args:
    path (str): Path to be validated.
Returns:
    Path: Path object.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker._process_parsed_docling_json
documentation:
Process the parsed docling json file and return a dataset.
Args:
    json_fp (Path): Path to the parsed docling json file.
Returns:
    List: a list of chunks built from the provided json file

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.fuse_texts
documentation:
Fuse short texts with preceding longer texts if their token count is below the threshold.
Args:
    text_list (list): List of text chunks to process.
    short_length_threshold (int): The token count threshold for determining short texts.
                              Default is 130, tuned specifically for the Mixtral tokenizer.
                              Update this value if changing the tokenizer model.
Returns:
    list: List of fused texts.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.create_tokenizer
documentation:
Create a tokenizer instance from a pre-trained model or a local directory.

Args:
    model_name (str): The name of the model or the path to the local directory.

Returns:
    AutoTokenizer: The tokenizer instance.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.get_token_count
documentation:
Get the number of tokens in a text using the provided tokenizer.
Args:
    text (str): The text to tokenize.
    tokenizer (AutoTokenizer): The tokenizer to use.
Returns:
    int: Number of tokens.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.add_heading_formatting
documentation:
Add heading formatting to the text if the first part is short.
Args:
    text (str): The input text to format.
Returns:
    str: Formatted text with headings applied.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.generate_table_from_parsed_rep
documentation:
Generate the table from the parsed representation and return as a string.
Args:
    item (dict): Parsed representation of a table.
Returns:
    str: Formatted table as a string.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.get_table
documentation:
Retrieve a table from a document based on a reference string.
Args:
    json_book (dict): JSON representation of the document.
    table_ref (str): Reference path to the table within the document.
Returns:
    str: Formatted table string.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.get_table_page_number
documentation:
Get the page number of a table or other document element.
Args:
    json_book (dict): JSON representation of the document.
    idx (int): Index of the element in the document.
Returns:
    int: Page number of the element.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.build_chunks_from_docling_json
documentation:
Build document chunks from a docling JSON representation.
Args:
    json_book (dict): JSON document to process.
    max_token_per_chunk (int): Maximum token count per chunk.
    tokenizer (AutoTokenizer): Tokenizer instance to use.
    keep_same_page_thing_together (bool): Whether to keep content on the same page together.
    chunking_criteria (callable): Custom function for determining chunk breaks.
Returns:
    list: List of document chunks.

methods.name: instructlab.sdg.utils.chunkers.ContextAwareChunker.export_documents
documentation:
Write converted documents to json files

Check for successful conversions and write those to the docling artifacts directory.
Returns:
    Path: path to directory with docling json artifacts
